fr.elements: Main Building; Roof System; Landscaped Area; Pedestrian Walkways; Perimeter Fencing
fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency response capabilities on the property could be achieved by implementing a few key measures. Trimming and managing the dense vegetation would assist in providing clearer visibility and access for responders, potentially improving their ability to navigate and assess the property quickly. Introducing wider or additional access points along the perimeter fencing where possible may facilitate easier entry for emergency vehicles and personnel, especially given the limited side access observed. Clearly designating and maintaining pedestrian walkways could further support the safe and rapid movement of responders throughout the property. These adjustments may collectively enhance the efficiency and safety of emergency operations.

wildfire.conditions: Tree Cluster; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Scattered Trees; Limited Defensible Space; Vegetation Adjacent to Access Route; Vegetated Perimeter; Vegetation Overhang; Dense Vegetation Corridor
wildfire.recommendations: Increasing the separation between the structure and adjacent vegetation as well as maintaining space between tree clusters and scattered trees could enhance defensible space. Managing the vegetation overhang near the structure and reducing dense vegetation corridors adjacent to access routes may improve access for firefighters and allow more effective suppression efforts. Regular maintenance of the vegetated perimeter and limiting vegetation concentrations around all structures could further support property resilience by minimizing fuel continuity. Maintaining cleared, open spaces around the improvement through ongoing management practices may also aid in protecting the property in the long term. Focusing on vegetation management near the access route to keep it clear of obstructions may facilitate ease of entry for emergency services, further ensuring ease of access during wildfire events.
